tax: Placed at order rank, Fulgurant Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a charged-air body, not a plain moving-air one. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct charge mode with its own build-up source and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an arc-veined cloud or a clinging glowing mote. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — arc-scorch and ozone, or a fizzing static residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Fulgurant Elementals occupies charged storm-shafts and dry static galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates a charged-air pocket or a surface-static build-up and recur with the shaft or gallery re-charges, sustaining themselves on atmospheric or surface electrical charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members hide as charged mist or a faint glow and rouse to grounding metal or contact, striking or shocking. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a charged-air body, immunity to electricity and physical attack, and a grounding or damp failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: lightning strikes, stunning discharge, and contact-shock. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Fulgurant Elementals matters because they make charged galleries a strike-and-shock hazard wherever metal or damp invites the charge. It lets survey reports sort charge hazards by mode, so a crew knows whether to stow metal or dry-wax their lines without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Fulgurant Elementals is the order-rank grouping of air elementals whose bodies carry electrical charge within the True Elementals. Its members share a charged air body that hunts by discharge and bleeds away when grounded or wetted, and divide below into family groups by whether the charge strikes from the air or crawls along surfaces. Fulgurant elementals are the storm kin of air, hazards wherever charge can build in dry galleries.
